steps.title: Use the circle inequality
steps.content: The circle has equation $(x+3)^2+(y-1)^2=25$. A point is in the interior when $$(x+3)^2+(y-1)^2\lt25.$$ The Desmos graph marks the circle and the answer-choice points so you can see which point is outside before confirming algebraically. A point is not in the interior if the value is $25$ or greater.

steps.title: Use the equation to verify the graph
steps.content: For a point to be inside the circle, $(x+3)^2+(y-1)^2\lt 25$. Choice A gives $(-4)^2+2^2=20$, choice B gives $0$, and choice C gives $3^2+(-1)^2=10$; all three are less than $25$, so they are inside.

steps.title: Choose the outside point
steps.content: Choice D, $(3,2)$, gives $(3+3)^2+(2-1)^2=36+1=37$, which is greater than $25$. It is outside the circle, so it is not in the interior. Choice D is correct.
